repo_notes.content: The project is an Excel-based wrapper for Graphviz. Every documentation page must respect the distinction between Excel (the UI and Data layer) and Graphviz (the rendering engine).
Technical Consistency: Always refer to the primary data entry points as 'Items' and 'Related Items' rather than 'From' and 'To' to match the worksheet headers.

Cross-Platform Logic: When documenting any code that triggers a shell command or file input output, mention the conditional logic for Windows (Windows Shell via CreatePipe) vs. macOS (AppleScript bridge).
Module Mapping: Every functional feature should be linked to its corresponding VBA module (e.g., modRibbonTab*.bas for UI, modCreateGraph.bas for parsing, Graphviz.cls for execution).
State Management: Remind that the tool relies on a 'Settings' worksheet for persistent state and a global 'internalMyRibbon' variable for UI state.
Error Handling Philosophy: Emphasize that the tool uses 'ValidateData' and local error handling to prevent VBA global state loss, which would break the Ribbon reference.
Graphviz Syntax: When discussing attributes (color, shape, etc.), ensure they align with the official DOT language specifications.
Community Feedback: Mention that community feedback and feature requests are captured via GitHub issues, and via Giscus-powered comments on the official website blog pages.

Version Awareness: Reference v8.0+ enhancements including Style Designer galleries, Graphviz.cls refactor, SQL connection pooling, unlimited CLUSTERn, SVG editor form, silent mode, and new ribbon tabs (Info, Console, Diagnostics, Styles).

Common Pitfalls and Best Practices: Include Graphviz node ID rules (must start with letter or underscore, quote digits), layout reproducibility tips (start=regular), SVG image embedding via base64 in post-processor, and named-range contract for safe sheet modifications.

Project structure: All VBA source files are organized in CamelCase directories (ClassModules, MicrosoftExcelObjects, Modules, Forms). Exclusions intentionally drop the analysis-noise: the MicrosoftExcelObjects worksheet code-behind (including the per-locale data sheets), the vendored JsonConverter.bas (third-party VBA-JSON library), and the generic Dictionary.cls/Stopwatch.cls helpers. The architecturally significant classes (Graphviz.cls execution wrapper, Cluster.cls, Style.cls) remain indexed.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: High-level entry point outlining the project's mission, key features, and architecture.

page_notes.content: Core Mission: Transforms structured Excel data (Items and Related Items) into professional Graphviz-rendered diagrams with zero DOT coding required.
Key Capabilities: Ribbon-based Style Designer, SQL-driven graph generation, dynamic AutoDraw reactivity, and multi-format publishing.
Architectural Flow: Data Entry (Worksheet) → Parsing (modCreateGraph.bas) → DOT generation → Graphviz rendering → Image display in Excel.

pages.title: Getting Started and Installation
pages.purpose: Onboarding guide covering setup, dependencies, and cross-platform compatibility.

page_notes.content: Graphviz Dependency: Install Graphviz and ensure the dot executable is in the system PATH.
Macro Security: Unblock the file in Windows properties and enable macros in Excel. Review public source on GitHub before enabling.
macOS Specifics: Uses AppleScript bridge for shell execution; SQL features are Windows-only due to ADO requirements.

pages.title: Core Concepts and Terminology
pages.purpose: Foundational glossary of graph terms and project-specific concepts.

page_notes.content: Item vs Related Item: Excel worksheet terms defining the endpoints of edges.
Subgraphs vs Clusters: Both group nodes; Clusters (prefixed cluster_) render with a visible bounding box in Graphviz.

pages.title: Architecture and Data Flow
pages.purpose: High-level technical map of the system's layers and transformation pipeline.

page_notes.content: Transformation Pipeline: Worksheet data → modCreateGraph.bas parsing → DOT file → Graphviz executable → output image (or worksheet).
Multi-Tiered Layers: Excel UI and Data, VBA Logic (modules + classes), and External Graphviz engine.

pages.title: VBA Class Hierarchy and Key Objects
pages.purpose: Overview of the object-oriented core and data structures.

page_notes.content: Key Classes: Graphviz.cls (execution), Cluster.cls, Style object model, and dataRow UDT as worksheet-to-DOT intermediary.
Integration: Classes work with named ranges and modCreateGraph.bas while protecting Ribbon state via local error handling.

pages.title: Graph Generation Pipeline
pages.purpose: End-to-end lifecycle from worksheet parsing to rendering.

page_notes.content: Row Classification: modCreateGraph.bas handles TYPE_NODE, TYPE_EDGE, and TYPE_SUBGRAPH rows.
File Handshake: Writes DOT string to temp file and invokes Graphviz via platform-specific execution.

pages.title: Graphviz Class and Process Execution
pages.purpose: Low-level execution logic for communicating with Graphviz binaries.

page_notes.content: Windows Path: Uses modExecuteAndCapture.bas with CreatePipe, CreateProcessA, PeekNamedPipe, and ReadFile routines for deadlock-free stdout and stderr capture.
macOS Path: AppleScript bridge. Graphviz.cls refactor centralizes cross-platform logic.

pages.title: Worksheet Architecture and Named Ranges
pages.purpose: Guide to sheet structure and the named-range contract.

page_notes.content: Named Range API: Over 700 named ranges act as a stable contract, allowing UI changes without breaking VBA code.
Maintenance Note: Avoid hard-coded cell addresses; always use named ranges.

pages.title: Constants, Data Types and Utility Modules
pages.purpose: Foundational building blocks and utility organization.

page_notes.content: Constant Prefixes: SETTINGS_ and DESIGNER_ map directly to named ranges.
Utilities: String helpers, picture management, OptimizeCode_Begin, OptimizeCode_End, and ADO connection pooling.

pages.title: User Interface Ribbon Tabs
pages.purpose: Overview of custom Ribbon architecture and callback system.

page_notes.content: Callback Model: Dedicated modules (e.g., modRibbonTabGraphviz.bas) bridge UI to logic.
State Management: Global internalMyRibbon + InvalidateRibbonControl pattern. Includes newer tabs: Info, Console, Diagnostics, Styles.

pages.title: Graphviz Ribbon Tab
pages.purpose: Guide to layout engines and publishing functions.

page_notes.content: Layout Engines: dot, neato, fdp, sfdp, twopi, circo, osage, patchwork.
Publish Group: graphToWorksheet, graphToFile, graphAllViewsToFile.

pages.title: Style Designer Ribbon Tab
pages.purpose: Technical guide to the Style Designer and gallery system.

page_notes.content: Gallery Previews: Uses ChartObjects for color swatches and Graphviz for font previews (v8.0+ enhancements).
RestoreStyleDesigner: Re-hydrates ribbon settings when a style is selected.

pages.title: SQL Ribbon Tab
pages.purpose: Technical guide to the SQL-driven graph generation and connection management.

page_notes.content: Query Engine: Uses modWorksheetSQL.bas and modWorksheetSQLClusters.bas to execute user-defined SQL against external databases or local Excel tables via ADO.
Dynamic Mapping: SQL results are mapped to 'Item' and 'Related Item' fields to feed the Graphviz pipeline automatically.
Connection Pooling: Leverages a centralized connection string managed in code, which finds the best Provider for the user's OS and software.
Floating Action Button (FAB): Explains the 'Run SQL' overlay that allows for rapid iterative testing of graph queries.
Platform Limitation: Clearly state that SQL features are Windows-only due to dependency on ActiveX Data Objects (ADO).

pages.title: Working with the Data Worksheet
pages.purpose: Guide to data entry and event-driven behavior.

page_notes.content: AutoDraw Reactivity: Worksheet_Change triggers live graph updates.
SelectionChange: Updates ribbon context and style dropdowns. Uses ValidateData for safe error handling.

pages.title: Defining Nodes and Edges
pages.purpose: Node and Edge data models with advanced syntax.

page_notes.content: Advanced Support: Port syntax (node:port), HTML-like labels in angle brackets, and GetDataRow structure.

pages.title: Subgraphs and Clustering
pages.purpose: Hierarchical grouping and stack-based parsing.

page_notes.content: Parsing Logic: Stack-based indentation in ProcessSubgraphOpen (modCreateGraph.bas).
SQL Clustering: Unlimited CLUSTERn fields and {label} placeholder support (v10.3+).

pages.title: Styles and the Style Gallery
pages.purpose: Style object model, caching, and preview lifecycle.

page_notes.content: Preview Mechanics: WIA for sizing on Windows; gallery-based previews with auto-refresh.

pages.title: Views: Filtering Graph Content
pages.purpose: View-based filtering and batch export.

page_notes.content: Batch Export: graphAllViewsToFile iterates defined View columns for mass generation.

pages.title: SQL Data Integration
pages.purpose: Windows-only ADO-based external data connectivity.

page_notes.content: Windows-Only: SQL ADO unavailable on macOS.
Supported Sources: External Excel workbooks and Access databases.

pages.title: SQL Engine and Connection Pooling
pages.purpose: ADO execution engine details.

page_notes.content: Pooling: modUtilityADODBConnectionPool.bas maintains handles for performance.
Late-Binding: Uses modUtilityADODBConstants.bas to avoid explicit library references.

pages.title: Advanced SQL Patterns
pages.purpose: Specialized query modes and recent enhancements.

page_notes.content: RecursiveSearch: Anchor and recursive member pattern for tree traversal.
Enhancements: Placeholders, concatenation, iterative queries, enumeration queries, CREATE EDGES, CREATE RANK, and multi-level CLUSTERn.

pages.title: Output, Publishing and Post-Processing
pages.purpose: Rendering, publishing, and SVG manipulation.

page_notes.content: SVG Post-Processor: XML find and replace for JS injection for interactivity and base64 image embedding (Excel reliability).
Formats: EMF for Office integration; SVG editor form available for manual tweaks.

pages.title: Event-Driven Architecture and AutoDraw
pages.purpose: Worksheet events and real-time reactivity.

page_notes.content: Lifecycle: Worksheet_Change and Worksheet_SelectionChange drive AutoDraw while protecting internalMyRibbon via ValidateData.

pages.title: Settings and Diagnostics
pages.purpose: Configuration, health checks, and system state.

page_notes.content: GetSettings UDT: Cached repository for all configuration values.
Diagnostics Worksheet: Automated version and environment tests; supports silent mode.

pages.title: Troubleshooting, Best Practices and Graphviz Quirks
pages.purpose: Practical guidance for common issues.

page_notes.content: Node IDs: Start with letter or underscore; quote digits.
Diagnostics: Worksheet tests OS, Excel, and Graphviz versions; check Console for stdout and stderr messages.

pages.title: Data Exchange (JSON Import and Export)
pages.purpose: E2GXF format for portability and version control.

page_notes.content: Use Cases: Git-friendly templates and sharing via text files.
Implementation: modExchangeImport.bas handles carriage-return kludge for multi-line cells.

pages.title: Web Presence and Community
pages.purpose: Public ecosystem and contribution channels.

page_notes.content: Site: VitePress on GitHub Pages at exceltographviz.com with custom domain.
Feedback: GitHub issues and Giscus comments on blog posts. Public source encourages community contributions.
